title: Chirocalyx abyssinicus
intro: Chirocalyx abyssinicus, also known as Abyssinian calyx, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rutaceae and is native to the tropical regions of East Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems. The bioactive constituents of Chirocalyx abyssinicus include limonoids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
